tickets.title: US001: Student account verification
tickets.description:
As a student, I want to create an account using my .edu email address so that only verified students can access the marketplace.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. The system accepts registration using a valid .edu email address.
2. The system rejects registration using a non-.edu email address.
3. The user can access the marketplace after email verification.

tickets.title: US002: Safe exchange zone selection
tickets.description:
As a student, I want to select a safe exchange zone before chatting with a seller so that item exchanges are safer.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. The system displays a list of approved safe exchange zones.
2. The user must select a safe exchange zone before starting a chat.
3. The selected safe exchange zone is visible in the chat.

tickets.title: US003: Barcode scanning for listings
tickets.description:
As a student, I want to scan a textbook barcode with my phone camera so that I can create listings faster without manually entering details.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. The student can scan a barcode using the device camera.
2. The system automatically fills in available textbook information after scanning.
3. The student can edit the listing information before publishing.

tickets.title: US004: Safe exchange zone management
tickets.description:
As a staff member, I want to manage approved Safe Exchange Zones so that students can meet in secure locations for transactions.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. The staff member can add a new Safe Exchange Zone.
2. The staff member can edit or remove an existing Safe Exchange Zone.
3. Students can view the updated list of approved Safe Exchange Zones when arranging meetups.

tickets.title: US005: Auction-based item listing
tickets.description:
As a student, I want to create auction listings for my items so that I can sell them to the highest bidder.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. The student can create a listing with an auction option.
2. Other students can submit bids higher than the current highest bid.
3. The system displays the current highest bid for the auction item.

tickets.title: NFR Epic 1: Security: Only users with verified .edu email addresses should be allo
tickets.description: Security: Only users with verified .edu email addresses should be allowed to create accounts.

tickets.title: NFR Epic 2: Safety: The system should promote safe exchanges by requiring users to
tickets.description: Safety: The system should promote safe exchanges by requiring users to select approved Safe Exchange Zones.

tickets.title: NFR Epic 3: Usability: Users should be able to quickly create listings using barco
tickets.description: Usability: Users should be able to quickly create listings using barcode scanning instead of manual data entry.

analysis.ambiguity1: The requirement that the app should be "exclusive" is too vague because it does not clearly define all eligibility rules beyond having a valid .edu email address. It does not specify how accounts should be verified or handled after graduation.
analysis.ambiguity2: The requirement to suggest Safe Exchange Zones is vague because it does not define how zones are selected, maintained, or whether users can choose alternative meeting locations. The requirement lacks clear operational rules.
analysis.conflictSolution: There is a conflict between the University Legal Team, which does not allow the app to process payments, and the Student Union President, who wants to collect a 2% transaction fee. The solution is to avoid in-app payment processing and instead allow optional donations to the Student Union through a separate university-approved donation system.
analysis.nfr1: Security: Only users with verified .edu email addresses should be allowed to create accounts.
analysis.nfr2: Safety: The system should promote safe exchanges by requiring users to select approved Safe Exchange Zones.
analysis.nfr3: Usability: Users should be able to quickly create listings using barcode scanning instead of manual data entry.
